title: Hisuian Samurott Battle Guide
summary: A [Water]/[Dark] physical attacker with [sharpness] — boosts slicing moves by 50%. The signature [ceaseless-edge] also lays hazards.
strengths: [ceaseless-edge] is a STAB + hazard in one move
bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Hisuian Samurott's identity is the signature move [ceaseless-edge] plus the [sharpness] ability. [sharpness] boosts slicing moves by 50%, and [ceaseless-edge] doubles as a hazard setter — it leaves splinters on the opponent's side that chip damage on switch. Together they form a rare 'attack + utility in one move' package.

## Strengths
[ceaseless-edge] STAB + [sharpness] + hazard setup is a three-in-one value. [razor-shell] also benefits from [sharpness] and applies a Defense drop, and [sacred-sword] covers [Normal], [Rock], and [Dark] walls. The dual [Water]/[Dark] STAB covers a wide threat spread.

## Weaknesses & Answers
Five weaknesses and average bulk mean switch-in reads matter. [Fighting] attackers threaten KOs, and [Electric]/[Grass] special attackers can punish fast. Speed 85 without support means [tailwind] is often required.

## Recommended Moveset
[ceaseless-edge] is the signature move. [razor-shell] is the [Water] STAB with a Def drop. [sacred-sword] is the [Fighting] coverage slicing move. [protect] is standard.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[life-orb] is the default. [choice-band] is the max-damage variant. [sharpness] is locked. Tera [Dark] stacks on [ceaseless-edge], Tera [Water] stacks on [razor-shell].

## Teammates & Synergy
Cover [Fighting] and [Bug] weaknesses with [Fairy] and [Fire] partners. Hazard-cycle teams benefit from the splinter utility on [ceaseless-edge]. [tailwind] support handles the Speed issue.

## Play Patterns
First turn is usually [ceaseless-edge] to set splinters and pressure the opponent into a pivot. [razor-shell] for Def drops, [sacred-sword] for coverage. Save Tera for a damage commit when closing the game.
